summary: Iteration 2: Configurable approval-workflow feature fully tested. 19 new pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/test_workflows.py (CRUD + RBAC + matching engine + preview + full-flow + leak checks) — 19/19 pass. Regression suite (iteration_1 test_hrms_backend.py) — 25/25 pass after one legacy test was updated (leave_type switched from 'casual' to 'earned') because the new engine now correctly resolves casual≤3d to the seeded 1-step workflow. Frontend: HR admin → Workflows page renders all 5 seeded workflows with correct tab grouping (Leave=2, Product & Service=3), New-workflow dialog creates a workflow end-to-end, Edit dialog updates name + adds step, Delete works with toast. No _id or password_hash leakage in any workflow endpoint.

issues: Missing aria-describedby on DialogContent — React console warning (accessibility nit, not blocking).

action_items: Optional accessibility polish: add aria-describedby (or <DialogDescription>) to the New/Edit approval workflow dialog to silence Radix warning.
critical_code_review_comments: approvals_routes._find_matching_workflow correctly ignores inactive workflows (is_active: True filter) and picks highest score; verified via toggle + fallback tests.

When a workflow matches but a resolver returns no user (e.g. no country_head exists), the step is silently skipped. This is intentional per the feature spec, but the final resolved step count may be smaller than the configured step count (Computer purchase — 5 levels seed resolves to 3 steps in current demo data). UI should ideally surface 'skipped steps' to the requester for auditability — currently workflow_name is stored but the skipped-step metadata is not persisted on the approval_request.

workflows_routes.update_workflow: merge returns `{**existing, **updates}` which drops the previous `id`/`company_id`/`created_at` fields from the payload correctly, but does not re-fetch from DB after update. For consistency a find_one after $set would be safer, though current tests confirm values are persisted.

workflows_routes.delete_workflow does not check if any approval_request references the workflow_id. Deleting a workflow leaves dangling workflow_name/workflow_id on historic approval_requests — acceptable for audit but consider soft-delete (is_active=false) as the preferred UX.
models.ApprovalWorkflowStep.resolver='user' requires user_id but there is no validation that the user exists or belongs to the same company_id. A cross-tenant user_id could be set via API.

Iteration_1 regression test test_leave_approval_end_to_end previously asserted 2 steps for casual leave; this was stale after the new engine. Updated to use leave_type='earned' which has no matching workflow, so the existing manager walk-up (mgr + HR admin) fallback runs — keeps the 2-step E2E regression intact.
test_credentials: hr@acme.io / Hr@12345 (company_admin), employee@acme.io / Employee@123, manager@acme.io / Manager@123 — all validated.
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up TEST_Furniture_*, TEST_UpdateWF_*, TEST_Toggle_*, TEST_Del_*, TEST_FE_WF_A workflows via test flows; seed data untouched (Stationery workflow restored after UI edit test).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two pytest files now guard HRMS backend: /app/backend/tests/test_hrms_backend.py (iteration 1 — 25 tests) + /app/backend/tests/test_workflows.py (iteration 2 — 19 tests). Run both with: pytest backend/tests/ -v. The workflow matching engine lives in /app/backend/routers/approvals_routes.py (_score_workflow + _find_matching_workflow). Seed workflows live in /app/backend/db.py::seed_demo_data. The only 5-step seeded workflow 'Computer purchase — 5 levels' currently resolves to 3 concrete steps in ACME demo data (no country_head / department_head users populated) — this is EXPECTED. Workflow_name is what must be asserted on approval_request, not step count.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found in this iteration. All requested tests passed. Only one legacy regression test had to be updated because its expectation (2-step manager+HR for casual leave) is now obsolete under the new configurable-workflow engine (casual≤3d resolves to 1-step manager-only by design).
